Math Project — Group 3

Bait Al
Rehman
Mosque

بيت الرحمن

This is our math project. We designed a mosque called Bait Al Rehman and calculated the cost to build it using real formulas — volume, surface area, unit rates, and a full BOQ. Everything is based on a 15×15m plot in the UAE.

View Full Report
2.02M AED Total Cost
225m² Built-up Area
26 Labour Workers
Project Model

About the Project

Project Info &
Dimensions

Project Name Bait Al Rehman Mosque بيت الرحمن
Plot Dimension 20m × 20m 400 m² total plot area
Built-up Area 15m × 15m 225 m² built-up footprint
Foundation Depth 1.5 m Reinforced concrete foundation
Wall Thickness 0.20 m 200mm structural masonry walls
Main Dome Diameter 5 m Reinforced concrete dome structure
Minaret Height 7 m 4 minarets, 1.5m × 1.5m base
Total Project Cost AED 2,016,746 All elements inclusive
Measurements

Key Dimensions

Plot Area 400 m²
Built-up Area 225 m²
Coverage Ratio 56.25%
Excavation Volume 337.5 m³
Foundation Volume 337.5 m³
Slab Volume 41.07 m³
Dome Curved Surface Area 39.27 m²
Wall Openings (Windows) 20 total
Main Doors 2 (2m × 3m each)
Minarets 4 (7m tall, 1.5m base)
Math Work

Surface Area & Volume
Calculations

Surface Area
Full formula steps with units for walls, openings, dome surface and paint coverage.

Wall perimeter = 4 × 15 m = 60 m. Wall height = 10 m. Gross wall area = 60 × 10 = 600 m².

Openings Windows = 20 × (1 m × 6 m) = 120 m². Doors = 2 × (2 m × 3 m) = 12 m². Total openings = 132 m².

Net wall area 600 − 132 = 468 m² for construction and paint.

Dome surface Curved dome area = 2πr² = 2 × π × 2.5² = 39.27 m². Dome base area = πr² = 19.63 m².

Paint area Exterior walls 468 + interior walls 468 + ceiling 225 = 1,161 m² total painting area.

Volume Work
Accurate cubic metre calculations with step-by-step formulas for concrete and structural volumes.

Foundation volume 15 m × 15 m × 1.5 m = 337.5 m³.

Slab volume Net slab area = 225 − 19.63 = 205.37 m². Slab thickness = 0.2 m. Slab volume = 205.37 × 0.2 = 41.07 m³.

Minaret volume Shaft volume = 4 × (7 m × 1.5 m × 0.2 m) = 8.4 m³. Cap volume = 4 × (1.5 m × 1.5 m × 0.2 m) = 1.8 m³.

Dome volume Hemisphere volume = 2/3 × π × 2.5³ = 32.72 m³ for concrete planning.

Paint & Materials
Realistic paint coverage and material quantities with full units and totals.

Paint coverage 1 L covers 10 m². Required area = 1,161 m². Two coats = 232 L. At 35 AED/L the paint cost is 8,120 AED.

Floor finish Marble floor = 225 m² × 900 AED/m² = 202,500 AED. Outside tiles = 175 m² × 100 AED/m² = 17,500 AED.

Concrete totals Foundation 337.5 m³ + slab 41.07 m³ + minarets 10.2 m³ + dome 32.72 m³ = 421.49 m³ total concrete.

Labour & Contingency
Detailed labour calculations and contingency coverage for the full project scope.

Monthly labour 26 workers × 1,200 AED = 31,200 AED per month.

Daily crew cost 31,200 ÷ 26 working days = 1,200 AED per day.

Worker rate 1,200 ÷ 26 = 46.15 AED per worker per day.

Contingency All unit rates include 10% contingency to account for materials, labour and finishes.

Note: This report includes full working steps, correct units, formulas, paint calculations, labour estimates, contingency and a realistic 53–55 week construction timeline.
Our Team

Our
Group

01
Ammar
Rabie
Calculations

Ammar did the structural calculations — foundation volumes, wall areas, dome geometry. He used πr² and 2πr² for the dome and checked that everything matched the actual dimensions.

02
Yousif
Omar
Excel & Costs

Yousif made the Excel sheet and calculated the cost for each item. He figured out the unit rates for concrete, steel, and labour, added 10% contingency, and got the total to around 2M AED.

03
Asadel
Rizal
Timeline

Asadel planned the construction order and split it into phases. He also worked out the labour costs — 26 workers at 1,200 AED/month and what that means per day.

Cost Table

All Items &
Their Costs

Calculation Method: We took the total wall area and subtracted the windows and doors. For the dome we used 2πr² for the curved surface and πr² for the flat base. Every unit rate includes materials, labour, tools, transport, and a 10% contingency on top.
SN Item Description Dims (L×W×t) Unit Nos Quantity Unit Rate (AED) Total Cost (AED)

3 Foundation (RC) 15 × 15 × 1.5 m m³ — 337.5 1,500 506,250
4–8 Construction Walls — Net (2 sides × wall area minus openings) 15×10×0.2m walls; 20 windows, 2 doors deducted m² 4 468 1,000 468,000
9–10 Slab (net of dome opening) 15 × 15 × 0.2 m m³ — 41.07 2,000 82,146
11 Dome — Curved Surface (2πr²) Radius 2.5 m m² — 39.27 2,500 98,175
12 Dome — Base/Cap (πr²) Radius 2.5 m m² — 19.63 2,500 49,088
13 Minarets — Shaft (7m × 1.5m × 0.2m) 7 × 1.5 × 0.2 m³ 4 8.4 2,500 21,000
14 Minarets — Cap (1.5m × 1.5m × 0.2m) 1.5 × 1.5 × 0.2 m³ 4 1.8 2,500 4,500
15 Main Doors — Construction (2 × 3m) 2 × 3 m each m² 2 — 10,000 20,000

17 Windows — 2 sides × 12 (1m × 6m) 1 × 6m each m² 12 72 1,200 86,400

Build Order

Construction
Timeline

A 12-month construction programme for the mosque, showing the monthly sequence of works from site preparation through to final handover.
Month Period Key Works
Month 1 June 2026 Site Preparation and Excavation Works
Foundation Works initiation
External Boundary Wall (Block + Plaster)
Month 2 July 2026 Completion of Foundation Works
Backfilling and Neck Column Works
External Boundary Wall (Continued)
Month 3 Aug 2026 Blockwork and Insulation for Substructure
Ground Tie Beam construction
Ground Floor Cleaning Slab (Screed)
Month 4 Sept 2026 Ground Floor Column Works
Ground Floor Roof Slab casting
Month 5 Oct 2026 First Floor Column Works
Second Floor Roof Slab casting
Septic Tank and Manhole construction
Month 6 Nov 2026 Parapet and Roof works
Ground Floor and First Floor Blockwork
Month 7 Dec 2026 Internal Plastering works
External Stone Cladding and External Plastering
Month 8 Jan 2027 Completion of External Stone Works
Electrical and Sanitary Rough-ins
Internal Plastering (Continued)
Month 9 Feb 2027 Windows and Painting Prep
Windows: Installation of arched aluminium frames and dome glazing
Painting: Surface preparation, primer, and first internal coat
Flooring: Floor screeding and leveling for prayer hall
Month 10 Mar 2027 Doors and Second Fix MEP
Doors: Installation of ornamental entrance doors and internal frames
MEP Second Fix: Installation of light switches and AC grills
Flooring: Final marble and tile installation
Month 11 Apr 2027 Decoration and Fit-out
Painting: Final decorative coats and gold/metal accents on dome
Fit-out: Installation of Mihrab, Minbar, and light fixtures
Electrical Final Fix: Chandeliers and external lighting
Month 12 May 2027 Final Works and Handover
External paving, landscaping, and final site cleaning
Commissioning: Testing sound systems, AC, and plumbing
Final inspection and project completion
This schedule represents the full 12-month construction programme for the mosque design, from site readiness to handover.
